inflation_a2_generate.rationale: Learners conflate CPI, PPI, and GDP deflator; a labelled comparison framework supports the comparison_table without completing it or answering prompts.
inflation_a2_generate.reasoning_supported: Discriminate price indices by coverage and typical misinterpretation — figure supports the table; does not replace row completion or newspaper prompt answer.
inflation_a2_generate.canonical_discipline_note: Introductory economics: label coverage (consumer basket vs domestic output) and what each index tracks — no currency or rising-arrow motifs.
must_show: CPI, PPI, and GDP deflator as distinct constructs
allowed_claims: GDP deflator relates to domestically produced goods and services
disallowed_claims: numeric index levels not stated in materials
inflation_a2_generate.caption_intent: Name what each index tracks; do not answer which measure to report to a student newspaper.

climate_mechanism_generate.rationale: Mechanism diagram reduces weather-vs-climate slogan confusion after cards, before learners complete evidence lines in the analysis template.
climate_mechanism_generate.reasoning_supported: Trace radiative balance / greenhouse mechanism before evidence weighing; do not label misconception cards true or false.
climate_mechanism_generate.canonical_discipline_note: Radiative balance schematic with explicit energy arrows; no political or disaster photography.
climate_mechanism_generate.source_basis: materials.task_cards; activity purpose on misconception discussion
must_show: energy inputs and outputs in balance framing
allowed_claims: greenhouse/radiative balance framing consistent with course materials
climate_mechanism_generate.caption_intent: Orient mechanism before for/against lines; no claim adjudication.

ci_a4_generate.rationale: Number-line segments reduce mental sketching of Control vs Treatment interval endpoints after learners work from the scenario table.
ci_a4_generate.reasoning_supported: Show overlap geometry for the two intervals using exact table endpoints; learner interprets importance in debrief — figure does not state verdict.
ci_a4_generate.canonical_discipline_note: Single shared axis; same units as scenario table; two labelled segments only.
allowed_claims: interval endpoints as listed in scenario table
ci_a4_generate.caption_intent: Depict endpoint positions and overlap only; learner judges strength of evidence.

inflation_a5_reject.rationale: Whole-group debrief uses discussion prompts only; a figure would summarize rather than support new reasoning — intentional no-visual.

inflation_a3_generate.rationale: Learners classify inflation scenarios by causal mechanism before completing the analysis table.
inflation_a3_generate.pedagogical_added_value: Adds discriminating cause-type cues and decision criteria for typing scenarios — not a blank copy of the learner analysis_table.
inflation_a3_generate.reasoning_supported: Learners classify inflation scenarios by causal mechanism without being given the completed classifications.
inflation_a3_generate.canonical_discipline_note: Use economics cause categories as empty labelled structures; do not imply all inflation has a single cause.
allowed_claims: Different causal mechanisms can produce inflation.
Classification depends on the driver of the price increase.
inflation_a3_generate.source_basis: A3 learner_task; A3 materials.scenarios; A3 materials.analysis_table
inflation_a3_generate.caption_intent: Support classification by showing the structure of inflation causes without completing the learner table.
